doc: Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_floor: round an MPFR value toward -∞ to an integer at the caller-supplied prec, returning {value, ternary}. No rnd parameter — direction is implicit (toward negative infinity). Behaviour: floor(2.7)=2, floor(-2.7)=-3 (more negative!), floor(0.3)=+0, floor(-0.3)=-1. Watch out: sign matters for floor — it's the SIGNED value that rounds toward -∞, not the magnitude. Class is 'arithmetic'.
divergence_from_c: C mutates rop; TS returns {value, ternary}. Behaviour matches mpfr_rint(r, u, MPFR_RNDD). NaN→NaN; ±Inf→±Inf; ±0→±0 (sign preserved); positive normal→trunc; negative normal with fractional bits→trunc - 1.
refs: mpfr/src/rint.c L341–L344 — mpfr_floor defers to mpfr_rint(r, u, MPFR_RNDD).
mpfr/src/rint.c L67–L72 — rnd_away = sign<0 for RNDD: floor of negatives rounds magnitude up (away from zero).
